archive_summary.title: Research History
archive_summary.description: Research-stage documents across completed tasks, plus core project context.
archive_summary.use_case: Literature review continuity, methodology discussion, and prior-investigation lookup.

section.title: Project Description
section.content:
# Electrophysiological Basis of Retinal Direction Selectivity
## Goal
Investigate how the internal electrophysiological properties of a single neuron shape its direction-selective function. Using a compartmental model of a direction-selective retinal ganglion cell, vary morphology, synaptic input density (AMPA and GABA), EPSP and IPSP amplitude and kinetics, combinations of somatic voltage-gated sodium and potassium conductances, and the presence or absence of voltage-gated conductances in the dendrites, then optimise these parameters against a target relationship between the angle of a spreading excitatory/inhibitory wave and the cell's action potential (AP) frequency.
## Scope
### In Scope
* Single-cell compartmental simulation (NEURON or equivalent) of a direction-selective retinal ganglion cell * Parametric variation of morphology, input density, EPSP and IPSP amplitude and kinetics, and combinations of voltage-gated sodium and potassium conductances * Comparison of active vs passive dendrites (presence or absence of voltage-gated conductances in the dendritic tree) * Wave stimulus protocols that sweep excitatory and inhibitory activation across the dendritic arbor at controlled angles * Optimisation of the Na/K conductance combination to match a target angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curve * In vitro patch-clamp data as validation ground truth
### Out of Scope
* Synaptic plasticity, learning, and developmental changes * In vivo electrophysiological recordings (only patch-clamp data are acceptable) * Network-level simulations beyond the single target cell * Non-retinal direction-selective systems (visual cortex, vestibular, etc.)
## Research Questions
1. Which combinations of somatic voltage-gated sodium and potassium conductances maximise AP frequency for a preferred-direction wave while suppressing firing in the null direction? 2. How sensitive is directional AP tuning to morphological parameters such as dendritic branching pattern and compartment length and diameter? 3. What effect do the ratio and spatial distribution of AMPA vs GABA input density have on the sharpness of directional tuning? 4. Do active dendritic voltage-gated conductances improve, degrade, or have no effect on the match to the target angle-frequency curve compared with passive dendrites? 5. How closely can the optimised single-cell model reproduce the target tuning curve, and what residual error remains?
## Success Criteria
* A modifiable compartmental model in which morphology, input density, EPSP and IPSP amplitude and dynamics, somatic Na/K conductances, and dendritic active conductances can each be varied independently * Identification of a Na/K conductance combination that reproduces the target angle-to-AP-frequency relationship within a defined error tolerance * Systematic comparison of active vs passive dendritic configurations on directional tuning sharpness * Reproducible simulation pipeline (seedable, scripted) that runs end-to-end on the researcher's local machine
## Key References
* Barlow & Levick (1965) — "The mechanism of directionally selective units in rabbit's retina", *J Physiol* (foundational paper defining retinal direction selectivity) * Hines & Carnevale (1997) — "The NEURON Simulation Environment", *Neural Computation* (canonical reference for the simulator used in this project) * Vaney, Sivyer & Taylor (2012) — "Direction selectivity in the retina: symmetry and asymmetry in structure and function", *Nature Reviews Neuroscience* (standard review of DS mechanisms) * Poleg-Polsky & Diamond (2016) — multi-compartmental NEURON model of an ON-OFF DSGC with distributed excitatory and inhibitory inputs (directly matches this project's modelling approach) * Oesch, Euler & Taylor (2005) — "Direction-selective dendritic action potentials in rabbit retina", *Neuron* (evidence for the functional role of active dendritic conductances in DSGCs) * Branco, Clark & Häusser (2010) — "Dendritic Discrimination of Temporal Input Sequences in Cortical Neurons", *Science* (cable-theory account of sequence-direction selectivity via dendritic impedance gradients and NMDA-receptor nonlinearities)
## Current Phase
The project is just starting. The next step is a literature survey of compartmental models of direction-selective retinal ganglion cells, together with selection of a published morphology and a target angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curve to use as the optimisation reference.

section.title: Completed Tasks and Results
section.content:
Completed tasks: 2
## Brainstorm results session 1 ID: t0001_brainstorm_results_1 Completed: 2026-04-18 Short description: Kickoff brainstorm: plan the first wave of tasks for the neuron-channels project. Result summary: Results Summary: Brainstorm Session 1 Summary First brainstorming session for the neuron-channels project. Produced four first-wave task folders (t0002-t0005) covering literature survey, simulator-library comparison, canonical target tuning curve generation, and DSGC morphology download. No suggestions were rejected, reprioritized, or created. Session Overview * **Date**: 2026-04-18 * **Context**: Run immediately after `/setup-project` completed. Project state was empty: no tasks, no suggestions, no answers, no costs, zero budget with no paid services. * **Prompt**: Phase 7 of `/setup-project` automatically chains `/human-brainstorm` to plan the first tasks. Decisions 1. **Create t0002...
## Literature survey: compartmental models of DS retinal ganglion cells ID: t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models Completed: 2026-04-19 Short description: Survey published compartmental models of direction-selective retinal ganglion cells to inform all five project research questions. Result summary: Results Summary: Literature Survey of Compartmental Models of DS Retinal Ganglion Cells Summary Produced a 20-paper survey of compartmental models of direction-selective retinal ganglion cells (DSGCs) covering all five project research questions, plus one synthesis answer asset that integrates the findings with per-RQ quantitative targets. The corpus includes all six seed references from `project/description.md` and 14 additional peer-reviewed papers spread across the five RQs, and it establishes concrete numerical targets (DSI **0.7-0.85**, preferred peak **40-80 Hz**, null residual **< 10 Hz**, half-width **60-90 deg**, **177 AMPA + 177 GABA** synapses, g_Na **0.04-0.10 S/cm^2**) that...

section.title: Planned Tasks
section.content:
Planned or active tasks: 3
## Simulator library survey for DSGC compartmental modelling ID: t0003_simulator_library_survey Status: not_started Effective date: 2026-04-18 Short description: Compare candidate compartmental simulators (NEURON, NetPyNE, Brian2, MOOSE, Arbor) and pick a primary plus backup.
## Generate canonical target angle-to-AP-rate tuning curve ID: t0004_generate_target_tuning_curve Status: not_started Effective date: 2026-04-18 Short description: Analytically generate a canonical cosine-like target tuning curve to use as the optimisation reference for all later fits.
## Download candidate DSGC morphology ID: t0005_download_dsgc_morphology Status: not_started Effective date: 2026-04-18 Short description: Download a reconstructed direction-selective ganglion cell morphology (SWC) for use as the project's baseline cell. Dependencies: t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models

section.title: Questions and Answers
section.content:
Answers: 1
## How does the existing peer-reviewed literature on compartmental models of direction-selective retinal ganglion cells structure the five project research questions (Na/K conductances, morphology sensitivity, AMPA/GABA balance, active vs passive dendrites, and angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curves), and what quantitative targets does it provide? ID: how-does-dsgc-literature-structure-the-five-research-questions Created by: t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models on 2026-04-18 Confidence: medium Categories: direction-selectivity, compartmental-modeling, retinal-ganglion-cell, dendritic-computation, synaptic-integration, voltage-gated-channels
The literature structures the five questions around a small set of quantitative targets that the project must hit. For Na/K conductances the Fohlmeister-Miller parameter set (peak somatic g_Na around 0.04-0.10 S/cm^2, delayed-rectifier g_K around 0.012 S/cm^2) is the standard starting point, and no published paper reports a factorial (g_Na, g_K) grid for DSGCs. For morphology the asymmetric ON-OFF DSGC dendrite is sharply wired in the null direction through SAC-mediated inhibition, yet global dendrite shape only minimally changes the synaptic map while local electrotonic compartments still matter. For AMPA/GABA balance the canonical counts on a reconstructed mouse DSGC are 177 AMPA and 177...

section.title: t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models — Literature survey: compartmental models of DS retinal ganglion cells — research_internet.md
section.content:
--- spec_version: "1" task_id: "t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models" research_stage: "internet" searches_conducted: 16 sources_cited: 26 papers_discovered: 22 date_completed: "2026-04-19" status: "complete" --- ### Literature Survey: Compartmental Models of Direction-Selective Retinal Ganglion Cells
#### Task Objective
Survey the published literature on compartmental models of direction-selective retinal ganglion cells (DSGCs) and the patch-clamp data that constrain them. The survey must cover all five project research questions (Na/K conductances, morphology sensitivity, AMPA/GABA balance, active-vs-passive dendrites, angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curves) and provide a shortlist of papers to download as paper assets for downstream tasks. It feeds task t0004 (target tuning-curve generator), task t0005 (morphology selection), and later Na/K optimisation and dendritic-conductance experiments.
#### Gaps Addressed
This is the project's first research task, so `research/research_papers.md` does not yet exist. The gaps below are derived directly from the five research questions in `project/description.md` and `task_description.md`. Each gap is resolved, partially resolved, or unresolved based on the internet search described in [Search Strategy](#search-strategy).
1. **RQ1 Na/K combinations (somatic conductances that reproduce directional AP firing)** — **Partially resolved**. The Fohlmeister-Miller family of RGC ion-channel models [Fohlmeister2010] is the community baseline, comprising somatic **I_Na, I_Ca, I_K (delayed rectifier), I_K,A (A-type), and I_K,Ca**. Recent DSGC-specific compartmental models [Schachter2010, PolegPolsky2016, Koren2017] retain this ion-channel complement but re-parameterise densities for direction selectivity. Precise published Na/K density pairs that robustly reproduce directional AP firing in DSGC morphologies are reported only for a handful of models; an explicit parametric sweep of (g_Na, g_K) combinations does not yet exist in the peer-reviewed literature.
2. **RQ2 morphology sensitivity (branching, diameter, compartment length)** — **Partially resolved**. [ElQuessny2021] used synaptically-matched compartmental simulations to show that varying rabbit/mouse DSGC dendritic morphologies produce only minor changes in the distribution of excitatory and inhibitory input magnitudes. [Briggman2011, Ding2016] provide serial electron-microscopy reconstructions of DSGC and starburst amacrine cell (SAC) morphologies. No single modelling paper gives a clean factorial sweep over branching pattern, diameter, and segment length.
3. **RQ3 AMPA/GABA balance (ratio and spatial distribution)** — **Resolved**. Quantitative E/I measurements are available: in mouse ON-OFF DSGCs excitatory currents are ~20% larger for preferred motion, inhibitory currents are **3-5x larger** for null motion [Park2014, Taylor2002]. [PolegPolsky2016] builds 177 AMPA + 177 GABA synapses onto a reconstructed DSGC, and NMDA receptors multiplicatively scale E by ~**2x** at high contrast.
4. **RQ4 active vs passive dendrites (voltage-gated conductances in DSGC dendrites)** — **Resolved**. [Oesch2005] recorded TTX-sensitive dendritic Na+ spikes in rabbit DSGC dendrites with patch-clamp and two-photon Ca2+ imaging; dendritic spikes are directionally tuned. [Schachter2010] built a NEURON compartmental model showing that dendritic Na+ channels amplify E/I-driven depolarisations non-linearly, **boosting direction-selectivity index (DSI) from ~0.3 (passive) to ~0.7 (active)**. [Sivyer2017] showed dendro-dendritic cholinergic excitation controls dendritic spike initiation.
5. **RQ5 angle-to-AP-frequency tuning curves (shapes, peak rates, half-widths, null suppression)** — **Resolved**. Multiple patch-clamp datasets provide quantitative tuning curves. Adult mouse ON-OFF DSGC peak firing rates **~40-80 Hz** (preferred), **~3-10 Hz** (null), giving DSI = (p-n)/(p+n) ~ **0.6-0.9** [Chen2009, Park2014]. Von-Mises fit half-widths ~**60-90 deg** in adult mouse [Chen2009]. Rabbit ON-OFF DSGCs: peak ~**50-100 Hz**, DSI ~**0.8-0.9** [Taylor2002, Sivyer2010].
#### Search Strategy
**Databases and sources searched**: Google Scholar, Semantic Scholar, PubMed/PMC, eLife, Nature Publishing Group, Cell Press (Neuron, Cell Reports), Journal of Neuroscience, Journal of Physiology, PLOS Computational Biology, ModelDB (senselab.med.yale.edu/modeldb), Webvision (NCBI Bookshelf).
**Queries executed (16 total)**, in exact form, across WebSearch and WebFetch:
Pass 1 — direct RQ-targeted queries:
1. `DSGC compartmental model NEURON simulation direction selective retinal ganglion` 2. `direction selective retinal ganglion cell sodium potassium conductance model somatic` 3. `starburst amacrine cell GABA DSGC direction selectivity inhibition asymmetric` 4. `ON-OFF direction selective ganglion cell tuning curve patch clamp null preferred direction` 5. `dendritic sodium spike DSGC Oesch active dendrite retinal` 6. `Poleg-Polsky Diamond direction selective compartmental model dendritic` 7. `Koch Poggio Torre retinal direction selectivity shunting inhibition model` 8. `retinal ganglion cell NEURON model morphology ModelDB compartmental`
Pass 2 — seed-reference verification and broadening queries:
9. `Fohlmeister Miller retinal ganglion cell sodium potassium calcium model conductance` 10. `Branco Clark Hausser dendritic direction selectivity NMDA sequence` 11. `Taylor Vaney DS retinal ganglion cell EPSC IPSC amplitude kinetics` 12. `Barlow Levick 1965 rabbit retina directionally selective units Journal Physiology`
Pass 3 — snowball queries prompted by Pass 1-2 results:
13. `DSGC dendrite morphology direction selectivity Vaney Sivyer Briggman` 14. `Hines Carnevale NEURON simulator 1997 Neural Computation` 15. `Sivyer Vaney off-DSGC dendritic morphology orientation mouse retina` 16. `Maturana Frech starburst amacrine cell compartmental model direction selectivity network`
Additional verification fetches used WebFetch against PubMed, PMC, Nature, eLife, and ModelDB URLs to confirm exact DOIs, authors, journals, and volumes for each discovered paper.
**Date range**: 1965 to 2025 for papers (foundational references included); no year cutoff for modelling tools or morphology datasets. Priority on 2000-2025 for synaptic data.
**Inclusion criteria**: Must provide at least one of (a) a compartmental DSGC (or closely related RGC/SAC) model with published conductance parameters, (b) quantitative patch-clamp tuning-curve or E/I measurements in DSGCs, (c) published DSGC morphology or dendritic reconstruction, or (d) a methodological reference (NEURON simulator, foundational DS paper) explicitly named in `project/description.md`. Excluded: purely behavioural studies, in vivo recordings, non-retinal DS systems (visual cortex, vestibular).
**Search iterations**: Queries 9-16 were follow-ups prompted by initial findings. Queries 9-12 targeted verification of seed references; 13-16 pursued morphological datasets, tool references, and SAC-network models that constrain the presynaptic inputs delivered to DSGCs.
**Paper corpus duplication check**: Running `uv run python -u -m arf.scripts.aggregators.aggregate_papers --format json --detail short` returns an empty corpus (the asset aggregator module does not yet exist in this worktree and no paper assets have been produced by any task). All 22 papers listed in [Discovered Papers] are therefore new to the project.
#### Key Findings
##### Canonical DSGC compartmental modelling pipeline
A consistent modelling pipeline has emerged in peer-reviewed DSGC literature. [Schachter2010] (PLOS Comp Biol) and [PolegPolsky2016] (Neuron) both use (i) an anatomically reconstructed DSGC morphology, (ii) somatic + dendritic Hodgkin-Huxley-style voltage-gated channels adapted from the Fohlmeister-Miller family [Fohlmeister2010], (iii) **~150-200 AMPA and ~150-200 GABA_A synapses** distributed across dendrites, and (iv) NEURON [Hines1997] as the simulator. [PolegPolsky2016] reports exactly **177 excitatory + 177 inhibitory synapses** on the DSGC dendritic tree, with NMDA receptors providing a **~2-fold multiplicative gain** on the excitatory conductance. The model code is publicly available at ModelDB entry 189347 [ModelDB-PP2016]. [Branco2010] (Science) provides the mechanistic logic — the product of a dendritic impedance gradient and voltage-dependent NMDA non-linearities generates sequence-direction selectivity even in cortical dendrites [Branco2010-MDB].
##### Na/K conductance baseline (RQ1)
The Fohlmeister-Miller lineage is the reference for RGC somatic conductance parameters [Fohlmeister2010]. The 2010 update calibrated the model against rat and cat RGC patch clamp data across **7-37 deg C** using multi-compartment simulations, reporting peak somatic g_Na ~**0.04-0.10 S/cm^2**, g_K (delayed rectifier) ~**0.012 S/cm^2**, g_K,A ~**0.036 S/cm^2**, and g_K,Ca ~**0.001 S/cm^2**, with sodium concentrated in an axonal sodium band **50-130 μm** from the soma. [Schachter2010] and [PolegPolsky2016] inherit this complement for the DSGC soma but increase dendritic g_Na to ~**0.015-0.025 S/cm^2** to sustain TTX-sensitive dendritic spikes reported by [Oesch2005]. The published literature does not cover a systematic (g_Na, g_K) grid search for DSGC directional tuning — this is the gap our task's downstream Na/K optimisation directly addresses.
##### Morphology sensitivity (RQ2)
[ElQuessny2021] performed a morphology-sensitivity test in eNeuro by swapping dendritic trees of multiple reconstructed mouse ON-OFF DSGCs while keeping input statistics constant. Their conclusion: overall DSGC dendritic morphology **minimally influences** the synaptic-input distribution and DS readout, consistent with the network-level view that direction is computed primarily by presynaptic asymmetries [Briggman2011, Ding2016]. However, local dendritic electrotonics still matter: [Schachter2010] partitions the dendritic tree into **separate electrotonic compartments** such that each region sums local inputs and fires locally thresholded spikes; increasing compartment length or diameter changes the spike-initiation threshold.
##### AMPA/GABA balance (RQ3)
Voltage-clamp measurements in mouse ON-OFF DSGCs consistently report: excitatory currents ~**20% larger** for preferred motion, inhibitory currents **3-5x larger** for null motion [Park2014, Taylor2002]. [Park2014] furthermore shows that mouse ON-OFF DSGC *excitatory* synaptic input **lacks direction tuning** — all direction selectivity seen at the spike output is built from directional GABAergic inhibition and local non-linearities. The asymmetric SAC-DSGC wiring that supplies this null-side GABA was established structurally by [Briggman2011] (SBEM) and extended to species comparisons by [Ding2016]. [Sethuramanujam2016] (Neuron) demonstrates that SACs co-release **acetylcholine and GABA** onto DSGCs, with ACh initiating responses in low-contrast natural scenes and glutamatergic excitation playing a secondary NMDA-amplified role.
##### Active vs passive dendrites (RQ4)
[Oesch2005] recorded TTX-sensitive dendritic Na+ spikes in rabbit ON-OFF DSGCs with patch-clamp and two-photon imaging. Dendritic spikes are directionally tuned and propagate to the soma. [Schachter2010] built a full NEURON model reproducing these spikes and showed that switching the dendritic tree from passive to active (adding g_Na and g_K) amplifies the somatic DSI from ~**0.3 to ~0.7**, primarily by thresholding local preferred-direction PSPs above null-direction PSPs. [Sivyer2017] (Nat Commun) showed that dendro-dendritic cholinergic input from SACs controls dendritic spike initiation. [Jain2020] (eLife) used two-photon Ca2+ imaging to show that individual mouse DSGC dendritic branches behave as semi-independent compartments. These are peer-reviewed results; cumulatively they argue strongly that **active dendritic conductances measurably sharpen directional tuning** but are **not strictly required** for direction selectivity (the presynaptic asymmetry alone is sufficient to produce a DSI of ~0.4-0.5, per [PolegPolsky2016]).
##### Tuning curves and quantitative targets (RQ5)
Usable quantitative targets, extracted from peer-reviewed patch-clamp datasets:
| Species | Cell type | Peak rate (pref) | Peak rate (null) | DSI | Half-width | Reference | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Rabbit | ON-OFF DSGC | ~50-100 Hz | ~5-15 Hz | 0.8-0.9 | ~60 deg | [Taylor2002, BarlowLevick1965] | | Rabbit | ON DSGC | ~20-40 Hz | ~2-8 Hz | 0.7-0.85 | ~80-90 deg | [Hoshi2011, Sivyer2010] | | Mouse (adult) | ON-OFF DSGC | ~40-80 Hz | ~3-10 Hz | 0.6-0.9 | ~60-90 deg | [Chen2009, Park2014] | | Mouse (P8-P12) | ON-OFF DSGC | ~10-30 Hz | ~2-6 Hz | 0.55-0.7 | comparable | [Chen2009] |
DSI is computed as `(preferred - null) / (preferred + null)` per [Chen2009]. Tuning widths are reported as full width at half maximum of a von-Mises fit to the angle-to-rate curve. A modelling DSI target of **0.7-0.85** with preferred-direction peak rate **~40-80 Hz** and null-direction residual **<10 Hz** is an appropriate optimisation reference.
##### Best practices emerging from the search
All findings below are drawn from peer-reviewed sources unless explicitly noted.
* **Best practice — simulator**: Use NEURON [Hines1997]. All the DSGC compartmental models found in this search ([Schachter2010, PolegPolsky2016, Koren2017]) use NEURON, and ModelDB contains a searchable directory of published models, including [Branco2010-MDB] and [ModelDB-PP2016] with freely available code. * **Best practice — morphology source**: Serial block-face EM reconstructions from [Briggman2011] and [Ding2016] provide full DSGC + SAC geometries; rabbit single-cell reconstructions are available via [Oesch2005] / [Schachter2010]. * **Best practice — ion-channel model**: Start from the Fohlmeister-Miller 2010 parameter set [Fohlmeister2010] (somatic I_Na, I_Ca, I_K, I_K,A, I_K,Ca) and modify dendritic g_Na to match [Oesch2005] spike amplitudes. * **Best practice — synaptic input**: Match the ~**177 AMPA + 177 GABA** distribution used in [PolegPolsky2016] unless there is a principled reason to deviate; add NMDA for multiplicative gain; optionally add nicotinic AChR per [Sethuramanujam2016]. * **Best practice — DS metric**: Report DSI per [Chen2009] and von-Mises half-width; split the angle-to-rate curve into ON and OFF components when the cell is ON-OFF.
##### Hypotheses to test in downstream tasks
* **H1**: A grid search of (g_Na, g_K) pairs will show a **ridge** of combinations that give DSI ≥ 0.7 — not a unique optimum. This is implied but not tested by [Fohlmeister2010] and [Schachter2010]. * **H2**: Switching the dendrites from passive to active with the Fohlmeister-Miller density will raise DSI by **~0.2-0.4** units on the same morphology and synaptic input, per [Schachter2010]. * **H3**: Scaling dendritic compartment diameter by 2x (keeping total area constant) will reduce dendritic spike initiation and therefore reduce DSI; reducing branching pattern (pruning higher-order branches) will have a **smaller** effect than diameter change, per [ElQuessny2021]. * **H4**: Increasing GABA/AMPA density ratio above the [PolegPolsky2016] baseline of 1:1 will sharpen the tuning curve (reduce half-width) at the cost of reducing preferred-direction peak rate.
##### Contradictions
None of the internet findings directly contradict the seed references in `project/description.md`. [ElQuessny2021] nuances the Vaney-Sivyer-Taylor [Vaney2012] view: they confirm that *global* DSGC dendritic morphology has only a **minor** effect on directional tuning when synaptic inputs are matched, which tempers any project expectation that morphology alone will drive RQ2 outcomes. The [Hanson2019] eLife paper partially challenges the classic SAC-centric model by showing that DS can persist in the absence of asymmetric SAC responses, pointing towards additional (parallel) mechanisms such as [Sethuramanujam2016] mixed ACh/GABA transmission.
#### Methodology Insights
* **Start from an available published model on ModelDB**, not from scratch. [ModelDB-PP2016] and [Branco2010-MDB] provide working NEURON code that can be re-parameterised on a published DSGC morphology. This aligns with rule 1 of the project CLAUDE.md (build on existing work). * **Use the Fohlmeister-Miller 2010 ion-channel complement as the RQ1 baseline**: I_Na, I_Ca, I_K, I_K,A, I_K,Ca with temperature scaling at 37 deg C [Fohlmeister2010]. Report conductance densities in **S/cm^2** per compartment, matching the published modelling convention. * **Use the [PolegPolsky2016] synapse budget** (177 AMPA + 177 GABA, optional NMDA multiplicative scaling, optional nicotinic ACh) as the RQ3 input baseline. Place inhibitory synapses biased to the null side of the DSGC per the structural evidence in [Briggman2011, Ding2016]. * **Parametrise morphology with three independent knobs**: number of branch orders, mean compartment diameter, and compartment length. Vary each while holding the other two fixed. [ElQuessny2021] suggests global morphology has a minor effect; the contribution of this project is a systematic factorial sweep not yet in the peer-reviewed literature. * **Use DSI and von-Mises half-width as the optimisation loss terms**. Target DSI **0.7-0.85**, preferred-direction peak rate **~40-80 Hz**, null-direction residual **< 10 Hz**, half-width **~60-90 deg** (mouse ON-OFF DSGC targets per [Chen2009, Park2014]). * **Benchmark the passive/active dendrite comparison** by running exactly the same morphology + synapse distribution with dendritic g_Na set to zero vs the [Schachter2010] value. Expect a DSI increase of **~0.2-0.4** with active dendrites. * **Cite ModelDB entry IDs** ([ModelDB-PP2016], [Branco2010-MDB]) in future plans and papers so reviewers can reproduce the model. ModelDB is not peer-reviewed per se, but every model on it links to a peer-reviewed paper. * **Pitfall to avoid**: do not mix [Fohlmeister2010] salamander and mammalian parameter sets; the 2010 paper is the mammalian (rat/cat) calibration and should be used for DSGC work.
#### Discovered Papers
All 22 papers below are new to the project's paper corpus (which is currently empty). Category slugs are chosen from `meta/categories/`: `cable-theory`, `compartmental-modeling`, `dendritic-computation`, `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `retinal-ganglion-cell`, `synaptic-integration`, `voltage-gated-channels`.
The six seed references from `project/description.md` (Barlow & Levick 1965, Hines & Carnevale 1997, Vaney/Sivyer/Taylor 2012, Poleg-Polsky & Diamond 2016, Oesch/Euler/Taylor 2005, Branco/Clark/ Häusser 2010) are listed first, followed by sixteen additional peer-reviewed papers discovered by this internet search.
##### [BarlowLevick1965]
* **Title**: The mechanism of directionally selective units in rabbit's retina * **Authors**: Barlow, H. B., Levick, W. R. * **Year**: 1965 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.1965.sp007638` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.1965.sp007638 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `retinal-ganglion-cell`, `patch-clamp` * **Why download**: Foundational paper defining retinal DS; establishes the sequence-discrimination inhibitory mechanism that underlies every subsequent DSGC model.
##### [Hines1997]
* **Title**: The NEURON simulation environment * **Authors**: Hines, M. L., Carnevale, N. T. * **Year**: 1997 * **DOI**: `10.1162/neco.1997.9.6.1179` * **URL**: https://direct.mit.edu/neco/article/9/6/1179/6087/The-NEURON-Simulation-Environment * **Suggested categories**: `compartmental-modeling`, `cable-theory` * **Why download**: Canonical reference for NEURON, the simulator used by every DSGC compartmental model cited in this task and by our planned implementation.
##### [Vaney2012]
* **Title**: Direction selectivity in the retina: symmetry and asymmetry in structure and function * **Authors**: Vaney, D. I., Sivyer, B., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2012 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nrn3165` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nrn3165 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `retinal-ganglion-cell` * **Why download**: Standard review of retinal DS mechanisms; frames the questions of RQ1-RQ5.
##### [PolegPolsky2016]
* **Title**: NMDA Receptors Multiplicatively Scale Visual Signals and Enhance Directional Motion Discrimination in Retinal Ganglion Cells * **Authors**: Poleg-Polsky, A., Diamond, J. S. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2016.02.013` * **URL**: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0896627316001069 * **Suggested categories**: `compartmental-modeling`, `direction-selectivity`, `synaptic-integration`, `dendritic-computation` * **Why download**: Multi-compartmental NEURON model of a mouse ON-OFF DSGC with 177 AMPA + 177 GABA synapses and NMDA multiplicative scaling. Directly matches this project's modelling approach; ModelDB code available.
##### [Oesch2005]
* **Title**: Direction-selective dendritic action potentials in rabbit retina * **Authors**: Oesch, N., Euler, T., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2005 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2005.06.036` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/fulltext/S0896-6273(05)00646-X * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `dendritic-computation`, `voltage-gated-channels`, `patch-clamp` * **Why download**: Evidence for TTX-sensitive dendritic Na+ spikes in rabbit DSGC dendrites; primary patch-clamp data for RQ4 (active vs passive dendrites).
##### [Branco2010]
* **Title**: Dendritic discrimination of temporal input sequences in cortical neurons * **Authors**: Branco, T., Clark, B. A., Häusser, M. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1126/science.1189664` * **URL**: https://www.science.org/doi/abs/10.1126/science.1189664 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `cable-theory`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Cable-theory account of sequence-direction selectivity via dendritic impedance gradient plus NMDA non-linearity; provides the mechanistic backbone for RQ2 and RQ4.
##### [Schachter2010]
* **Title**: Dendritic Spikes Amplify the Synaptic Signal to Enhance Detection of Motion in a Simulation of the Direction-Selective Ganglion Cell * **Authors**: Schachter, M. J., Oesch, N., Smith, R. G., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000899` * **URL**: https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000899 * **Suggested categories**: `compartmental-modeling`, `direction-selectivity`, `dendritic-computation`, `voltage-gated-channels` * **Why download**: Full NEURON compartmental model of a rabbit DSGC with active dendrites; quantifies the DSI gain from dendritic spikes. Direct template for our RQ1 and RQ4 experiments.
##### [Fohlmeister2010]
* **Title**: Mechanisms and distribution of ion channels in retinal ganglion cells: using temperature as an independent variable * **Authors**: Fohlmeister, J. F., Cohen, E. D., Newman, E. A. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1152/jn.00123.2009` * **URL**: https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/jn.00123.2009 * **Suggested categories**: `voltage-gated-channels`, `retinal-ganglion-cell`, `compartmental-modeling` * **Why download**: Mammalian-RGC ion-channel calibration (rat, cat) that every DSGC model in this survey inherits. Provides explicit somatic conductance densities for RQ1.
##### [Taylor2002]
* **Title**: Diverse synaptic mechanisms generate direction selectivity in the rabbit retina * **Authors**: Taylor, W. R., Vaney, D. I. * **Year**: 2002 * **DOI**: `10.1523/JNEUROSCI.22-17-07712.2002` * **URL**: https://www.jneurosci.org/content/22/17/7712 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Voltage-clamp measurement of preferred/null E and I currents in rabbit ON-OFF DSGCs; quantifies the 3-5x null-direction inhibition asymmetry for RQ3.
##### [Chen2009]
* **Title**: Physiological properties of direction-selective ganglion cells in early postnatal and adult mouse retina * **Authors**: Chen, M., Weng, S., Deng, Q., Xu, Z., He, S. * **Year**: 2009 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.2008.161240` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.2008.161240 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `retinal-ganglion-cell` * **Why download**: Quantitative DSI and tuning-width data for adult mouse ON-OFF DSGCs; defines the DSI formula used by the community and provides RQ5 target numbers.
##### [Park2014]
* **Title**: Excitatory synaptic inputs to mouse on-off direction-selective retinal ganglion cells lack direction tuning * **Authors**: Park, S. J. H., Kim, I.-J., Looger, L. L., Demb, J. B., Borghuis, B. G. * **Year**: 2014 * **DOI**: `10.1523/JNEUROSCI.5017-13.2014` * **URL**: https://www.jneurosci.org/content/34/11/3976 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Direct E/I measurement showing mouse ON-OFF DSGC excitation lacks direction tuning — DS is built from inhibition and local non-linearities. Key for RQ3.
##### [Briggman2011]
* **Title**: Wiring specificity in the direction-selectivity circuit of the retina * **Authors**: Briggman, K. L., Helmstaedter, M., Denk, W. * **Year**: 2011 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature09818` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature09818 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `retinal-ganglion-cell`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Serial block-face EM reconstruction proving that SACs on the null side of a DSGC make more inhibitory synapses than preferred-side SACs — structural basis of the RQ3 E/I spatial distribution.
##### [Ding2016]
* **Title**: Species-specific wiring for direction selectivity in the mammalian retina * **Authors**: Ding, H., Smith, R. G., Poleg-Polsky, A., Diamond, J. S., Briggman, K. L. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature18609` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature18609 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `retinal-ganglion-cell`, `compartmental-modeling` * **Why download**: Cross-species (mouse vs rabbit) SAC-DSGC wiring comparison with an anatomically-constrained SAC network model. Constrains species-specific synaptic input distributions for our DSGC model.
##### [Sivyer2010]
* **Title**: Synaptic inputs and timing underlying the velocity tuning of direction-selective ganglion cells in rabbit retina * **Authors**: Sivyer, B., van Wyk, M., Vaney, D. I., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.2010.192716` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.2010.192716 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Velocity-dependent E/I synaptic-input measurements in rabbit ON and ON-OFF DSGCs; useful for RQ5 tuning-curve targets at different speeds.
##### [Hoshi2011]
* **Title**: Two Distinct Types of ON Directionally Selective Ganglion Cells in the Rabbit Retina * **Authors**: Hoshi, H., Tian, L.-M., Massey, S. C., Mills, S. L. * **Year**: 2011 * **DOI**: `10.1002/cne.22678` * **URL**: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/cne.22678 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `retinal-ganglion-cell` * **Why download**: Morphological and physiological characterisation of ON DSGC types; useful for the RQ2 morphology shortlist (coupled vs uncoupled dendritic branching patterns).
##### [Koren2017]
* **Title**: Cross-compartmental modulation of dendritic signals for retinal direction selectivity * **Authors**: Koren, D., Grove, J. C. R., Wei, W. * **Year**: 2017 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2017.07.020` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(17)30650-3 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `direction-selectivity`, `compartmental-modeling` * **Why download**: Demonstrates cross-compartmental dendritic interactions in DSGCs; relevant to RQ2 (morphology) and RQ4 (active dendrites).
##### [ElQuessny2021]
* **Title**: Dendrite Morphology Minimally Influences the Synaptic Distribution of Excitation and Inhibition in Retinal Direction-Selective Ganglion Cells * **Authors**: El-Quessny, M., Feller, M. B. * **Year**: 2021 * **DOI**: `10.1523/ENEURO.0261-21.2021` * **URL**: https://www.eneuro.org/content/8/5/ENEURO.0261-21.2021 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `compartmental-modeling`, `direction-selectivity` * **Why download**: Direct simulation study testing how swapping DSGC dendritic morphologies affects E/I distribution; primary reference for RQ2 hypotheses.
##### [Jain2020]
* **Title**: The functional organization of excitation and inhibition in the dendrites of mouse direction-selective ganglion cells * **Authors**: Jain, V., Murphy-Baum, B. L., deRosenroll, G., Sethuramanujam, S., Delsey, M., Delaney, K. R., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2020 * **DOI**: `10.7554/eLife.52949` * **URL**: https://elifesciences.org/articles/52949 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging and patch-clamp data on dendritic E/I integration in mouse DSGCs; directly informs RQ3 and RQ4.
##### [Hanson2019]
* **Title**: Retinal direction selectivity in the absence of asymmetric starburst amacrine cell responses * **Authors**: Hanson, L., Sethuramanujam, S., deRosenroll, G., Jain, V., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2019 * **DOI**: `10.7554/eLife.42392` * **URL**: https://elifesciences.org/articles/42392 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Challenges the SAC-asymmetry-only model by showing DS persists when SAC responses are symmetric; useful counter-evidence for the RQ3 circuit assumptions.
##### [Sethuramanujam2016]
* **Title**: A Central Role for Mixed Acetylcholine/GABA Transmission in Direction Coding in the Retina * **Authors**: Sethuramanujam, S., McLaughlin, A. J., deRosenroll, G., Hoggarth, A., Schwab, D. J., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2016.04.041` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(16)30155-6 * **Suggested categories**: `direction-selectivity`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Shows SAC co-release of ACh + GABA on DSGCs; relevant for extending RQ3 input modelling beyond AMPA + GABA.
##### [Sivyer2017]
* **Title**: Dendro-dendritic cholinergic excitation controls dendritic spike initiation in retinal ganglion cells * **Authors**: Sivyer, B., Williams, S. R. (et al.) * **Year**: 2017 * **DOI**: `10.1038/ncomms15683` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms15683 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `voltage-gated-channels`, `direction-selectivity` * **Why download**: Demonstrates cholinergic control of dendritic spike initiation in RGCs; supports RQ4 modelling of active dendritic conductances.
##### [Euler2002]
* **Title**: Directionally selective calcium signals in dendrites of starburst amacrine cells * **Authors**: Euler, T., Detwiler, P. B., Denk, W. * **Year**: 2002 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature00931` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature00931 * **Suggested categories**: `dendritic-computation`, `direction-selectivity`, `patch-clamp` * **Why download**: Landmark discovery that SAC dendritic Ca2+ signals are themselves direction-selective; constrains the presynaptic signal delivered to DSGCs in RQ3 modelling.
##### [Enciso2010]
* **Title**: A model of direction selectivity in the starburst amacrine cell network * **Authors**: Enciso, G. A., Rempe, M., Dmitriev, A. V., Gavrikov, K. E., Terman, D., Mangel, S. C. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1007/s10827-010-0238-3` * **URL**: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10827-010-0238-3 * **Suggested categories**: `compartmental-modeling`, `direction-selectivity`, `synaptic-integration` * **Why download**: Compartmental model of the SAC network producing directional GABA waves that drive the DSGC; relevant for constraining the RQ3 input distribution.
#### Recommendations for This Task
1. **Download all 22 papers in [Discovered Papers]** as paper assets via the orchestrator's `/add-paper` pipeline. Priority order: seeds ([BarlowLevick1965, Hines1997, Vaney2012, PolegPolsky2016, Oesch2005, Branco2010]) first, then compartmental-modelling papers ([Schachter2010, Fohlmeister2010, Koren2017, ElQuessny2021, Enciso2010]), then E/I characterisation ([Taylor2002, Park2014, Sivyer2010, Chen2009, Sethuramanujam2016, Jain2020, Hanson2019, Euler2002]), then structural ([Briggman2011, Ding2016, Hoshi2011, Sivyer2017]).
2. **Adopt the [PolegPolsky2016] model as the project baseline**. Download the ModelDB 189347 code [ModelDB-PP2016] in the later implementation task and re-parameterise on a published reconstructed DSGC morphology.
3. **Use [Fohlmeister2010] parameters as the RQ1 starting point** for the Na/K optimisation grid search. Report results in the same (g_Na, g_K, g_K,A, g_K,Ca) parameterisation for future reviewer comparison.
4. **Use the quantitative targets from RQ5 as the optimisation loss**: target DSI **0.7-0.85**, preferred peak rate **~40-80 Hz**, null residual **<10 Hz**, half-width **~60-90 deg** for adult mouse ON-OFF DSGCs per [Chen2009, Park2014].
5. **Plan two paired dendritic-conductance experiments**: (a) passive dendrite (g_Na_dend = 0), (b) active dendrite with [Schachter2010, Fohlmeister2010] densities. Report the DSI delta.
6. **Cross-check species consistency**. Prefer mouse data ([Chen2009, Park2014, Ding2016, Jain2020]) over rabbit when setting quantitative targets, but retain rabbit morphology sources ([Oesch2005, Schachter2010]) for dendritic spike calibration; species differences in SAC-DSGC wiring [Ding2016] should be explicitly documented.
7. **Create a compare-literature asset for RQ5 tuning-curve targets** in the downstream task (t0004); pull the numerical targets directly from [Chen2009, Park2014, Taylor2002].
#### Source Index
##### [BarlowLevick1965]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: The mechanism of directionally selective units in rabbit's retina * **Authors**: Barlow, H. B., Levick, W. R. * **Year**: 1965 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.1965.sp007638` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.1965.sp007638 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Physiology) * **Relevance**: Foundational paper defining retinal direction selectivity and the sequence-discrimination inhibitory mechanism; ground truth for every downstream DSGC model.
##### [Hines1997]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: The NEURON simulation environment * **Authors**: Hines, M. L., Carnevale, N. T. * **Year**: 1997 * **DOI**: `10.1162/neco.1997.9.6.1179` * **URL**: https://direct.mit.edu/neco/article/9/6/1179/6087/The-NEURON-Simulation-Environment * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Neural Computation) * **Relevance**: Canonical reference for the NEURON simulator that every DSGC compartmental model in this survey uses and that our project will use.
##### [Vaney2012]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Direction selectivity in the retina: symmetry and asymmetry in structure and function * **Authors**: Vaney, D. I., Sivyer, B., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2012 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nrn3165` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nrn3165 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Nature Reviews Neuroscience) * **Relevance**: Standard review of DS mechanisms; frames all five project research questions and provides a road map to the primary literature.
##### [PolegPolsky2016]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: NMDA Receptors Multiplicatively Scale Visual Signals and Enhance Directional Motion Discrimination in Retinal Ganglion Cells * **Authors**: Poleg-Polsky, A., Diamond, J. S. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2016.02.013` * **URL**: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0896627316001069 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Neuron) * **Relevance**: Multi-compartmental NEURON model of a mouse ON-OFF DSGC with 177 AMPA + 177 GABA synapses and multiplicative NMDA gain; closest published match to our planned implementation.
##### [Oesch2005]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Direction-selective dendritic action potentials in rabbit retina * **Authors**: Oesch, N., Euler, T., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2005 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2005.06.036` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/fulltext/S0896-6273(05)00646-X * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Neuron) * **Relevance**: Primary patch-clamp + two-photon evidence for TTX-sensitive dendritic Na+ spikes in rabbit DSGC dendrites; RQ4 ground truth.
##### [Branco2010]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Dendritic discrimination of temporal input sequences in cortical neurons * **Authors**: Branco, T., Clark, B. A., Häusser, M. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1126/science.1189664` * **URL**: https://www.science.org/doi/abs/10.1126/science.1189664 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Science) * **Relevance**: Cable-theory basis for sequence-direction selectivity via dendritic impedance gradient + NMDA; informs RQ2 and RQ4.
##### [Schachter2010]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Dendritic Spikes Amplify the Synaptic Signal to Enhance Detection of Motion in a Simulation of the Direction-Selective Ganglion Cell * **Authors**: Schachter, M. J., Oesch, N., Smith, R. G., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000899` * **URL**: https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000899 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (PLOS Computational Biology) * **Relevance**: Full NEURON compartmental model of a rabbit DSGC with active dendrites; quantifies the DSI gain from dendritic spikes.
##### [Fohlmeister2010]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Mechanisms and distribution of ion channels in retinal ganglion cells: using temperature as an independent variable * **Authors**: Fohlmeister, J. F., Cohen, E. D., Newman, E. A. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1152/jn.00123.2009` * **URL**: https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/jn.00123.2009 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Neurophysiology) * **Relevance**: Mammalian-RGC ion-channel calibration — the g_Na, g_K complement used by every DSGC model cited here.
##### [Taylor2002]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Diverse synaptic mechanisms generate direction selectivity in the rabbit retina * **Authors**: Taylor, W. R., Vaney, D. I. * **Year**: 2002 * **DOI**: `10.1523/JNEUROSCI.22-17-07712.2002` * **URL**: https://www.jneurosci.org/content/22/17/7712 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Neuroscience) * **Relevance**: Voltage-clamp measurement of preferred/null E and I currents in rabbit ON-OFF DSGCs; quantifies the 3-5x null-direction inhibition asymmetry.
##### [Chen2009]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Physiological properties of direction-selective ganglion cells in early postnatal and adult mouse retina * **Authors**: Chen, M., Weng, S., Deng, Q., Xu, Z., He, S. * **Year**: 2009 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.2008.161240` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.2008.161240 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Physiology) * **Relevance**: DSI formula and adult mouse ON-OFF DSGC tuning-width data for RQ5 targets.
##### [Park2014]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Excitatory synaptic inputs to mouse on-off direction-selective retinal ganglion cells lack direction tuning * **Authors**: Park, S. J. H., Kim, I.-J., Looger, L. L., Demb, J. B., Borghuis, B. G. * **Year**: 2014 * **DOI**: `10.1523/JNEUROSCI.5017-13.2014` * **URL**: https://www.jneurosci.org/content/34/11/3976 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Neuroscience) * **Relevance**: Shows mouse DSGC excitatory input is untuned; inhibition is the DS driver. Key for RQ3 input modelling.
##### [Briggman2011]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Wiring specificity in the direction-selectivity circuit of the retina * **Authors**: Briggman, K. L., Helmstaedter, M., Denk, W. * **Year**: 2011 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature09818` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature09818 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Nature) * **Relevance**: Serial block-face EM reconstruction showing null-side SAC-DSGC wiring bias; structural basis of the RQ3 E/I spatial distribution.
##### [Ding2016]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Species-specific wiring for direction selectivity in the mammalian retina * **Authors**: Ding, H., Smith, R. G., Poleg-Polsky, A., Diamond, J. S., Briggman, K. L. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature18609` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature18609 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Nature) * **Relevance**: Mouse-vs-rabbit SAC-DSGC wiring comparison with an anatomically-constrained network model; constrains species-specific synaptic input distributions.
##### [Sivyer2010]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Synaptic inputs and timing underlying the velocity tuning of direction-selective ganglion cells in rabbit retina * **Authors**: Sivyer, B., van Wyk, M., Vaney, D. I., Taylor, W. R. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1113/jphysiol.2010.192716` * **URL**: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/jphysiol.2010.192716 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Physiology) * **Relevance**: Velocity-tuning synaptic-input data in rabbit ON and ON-OFF DSGCs; supports RQ5.
##### [Hoshi2011]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Two Distinct Types of ON Directionally Selective Ganglion Cells in the Rabbit Retina * **Authors**: Hoshi, H., Tian, L.-M., Massey, S. C., Mills, S. L. * **Year**: 2011 * **DOI**: `10.1002/cne.22678` * **URL**: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/cne.22678 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Comparative Neurology) * **Relevance**: Morphological + physiological characterisation of ON DSGC subtypes; useful for RQ2 morphology shortlist.
##### [Koren2017]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Cross-compartmental modulation of dendritic signals for retinal direction selectivity * **Authors**: Koren, D., Grove, J. C. R., Wei, W. * **Year**: 2017 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2017.07.020` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(17)30650-3 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Neuron) * **Relevance**: Cross-compartmental dendritic computation in DSGCs; informs RQ2 (morphology) and RQ4 (active dendrites).
##### [ElQuessny2021]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Dendrite Morphology Minimally Influences the Synaptic Distribution of Excitation and Inhibition in Retinal Direction-Selective Ganglion Cells * **Authors**: El-Quessny, M., Feller, M. B. * **Year**: 2021 * **DOI**: `10.1523/ENEURO.0261-21.2021` * **URL**: https://www.eneuro.org/content/8/5/ENEURO.0261-21.2021 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (eNeuro) * **Relevance**: Direct test of DSGC morphology sensitivity via simulation swaps; primary reference for RQ2 hypotheses.
##### [Jain2020]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: The functional organization of excitation and inhibition in the dendrites of mouse direction-selective ganglion cells * **Authors**: Jain, V., Murphy-Baum, B. L., deRosenroll, G., Sethuramanujam, S., Delsey, M., Delaney, K. R., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2020 * **DOI**: `10.7554/eLife.52949` * **URL**: https://elifesciences.org/articles/52949 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (eLife) * **Relevance**: Two-photon Ca2+ imaging + patch-clamp data on dendritic E/I integration in mouse DSGCs; informs RQ3 and RQ4.
##### [Hanson2019]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Retinal direction selectivity in the absence of asymmetric starburst amacrine cell responses * **Authors**: Hanson, L., Sethuramanujam, S., deRosenroll, G., Jain, V., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2019 * **DOI**: `10.7554/eLife.42392` * **URL**: https://elifesciences.org/articles/42392 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (eLife) * **Relevance**: Counter-evidence showing DS can persist without asymmetric SAC responses; nuances the RQ3 circuit model.
##### [Sethuramanujam2016]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: A Central Role for Mixed Acetylcholine/GABA Transmission in Direction Coding in the Retina * **Authors**: Sethuramanujam, S., McLaughlin, A. J., deRosenroll, G., Hoggarth, A., Schwab, D. J., Awatramani, G. B. * **Year**: 2016 * **DOI**: `10.1016/j.neuron.2016.04.041` * **URL**: https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(16)30155-6 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Neuron) * **Relevance**: SAC co-release of ACh + GABA onto DSGCs; expands RQ3 input taxonomy beyond AMPA + GABA.
##### [Sivyer2017]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Dendro-dendritic cholinergic excitation controls dendritic spike initiation in retinal ganglion cells * **Authors**: Sivyer, B., Williams, S. R. (et al.) * **Year**: 2017 * **DOI**: `10.1038/ncomms15683` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms15683 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Nature Communications) * **Relevance**: Cholinergic control of dendritic spike initiation in RGCs; supports RQ4 active dendrite modelling.
##### [Euler2002]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: Directionally selective calcium signals in dendrites of starburst amacrine cells * **Authors**: Euler, T., Detwiler, P. B., Denk, W. * **Year**: 2002 * **DOI**: `10.1038/nature00931` * **URL**: https://www.nature.com/articles/nature00931 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Nature) * **Relevance**: Discovery that SAC dendritic Ca2+ signals are themselves directional; constrains the presynaptic signal delivered to DSGCs.
##### [Enciso2010]
* **Type**: paper * **Title**: A model of direction selectivity in the starburst amacrine cell network * **Authors**: Enciso, G. A., Rempe, M., Dmitriev, A. V., Gavrikov, K. E., Terman, D., Mangel, S. C. * **Year**: 2010 * **DOI**: `10.1007/s10827-010-0238-3` * **URL**: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10827-010-0238-3 * **Peer-reviewed**: yes (Journal of Computational Neuroscience) * **Relevance**: Compartmental SAC-network model producing directional GABA waves that drive the DSGC; constrains RQ3 input distribution.
##### [ModelDB-PP2016]
* **Type**: repository * **Title**: Multiplication by NMDA receptors in Direction Selective Ganglion cells (Poleg-Polsky & Diamond 2016) * **Author/Org**: Poleg-Polsky, A., Diamond, J. S. (hosted on ModelDB, SenseLab, Yale University) * **Date**: 2016 * **URL**: https://modeldb.science/189347 * **Peer-reviewed**: no (accompanies the peer-reviewed [PolegPolsky2016] paper) * **Relevance**: NEURON source code for the [PolegPolsky2016] DSGC model — 177 AMPA + 177 GABA synapses, NMDA non-linearity, somatic + dendritic I_Na and I_K. Ready-to-reuse starting point for our implementation.
##### [Branco2010-MDB]
* **Type**: repository * **Title**: Dendritic Discrimination of Temporal Input Sequences (Branco et al. 2010) * **Author/Org**: Branco, T., Clark, B. A., Häusser, M. (hosted on ModelDB, SenseLab, Yale University) * **Date**: 2010 * **URL**: https://modeldb.science/140828 * **Peer-reviewed**: no (accompanies the peer-reviewed [Branco2010] paper) * **Relevance**: NEURON code implementing dendritic sequence-direction selectivity via NMDA non-linearity; reference implementation for the mechanistic core of RQ2 and RQ4.
##### [Webvision-DSGC]
* **Type**: documentation * **Title**: The Anatomy and Physiology of Direction-Selective Retinal Ganglion Cells * **Author/Org**: Liu, J. (Webvision, University of Utah / NCBI Bookshelf) * **Date**: 2018 (last major revision) * **URL**: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK321299/ * **Peer-reviewed**: no (open educational resource, edited but not formally peer-reviewed) * **Relevance**: Comprehensive summary of DSGC anatomy, physiology, morphology, and circuit organisation with references to the primary literature. Useful as a navigation index for the project's broader literature landscape; individual facts should be traced back to the primary sources cited above rather than cited from Webvision alone.
